subject: UX & Research
title: The Bing Blue Experiment
description: A 60-second lesson on The Bing Blue Experiment.

hook.text: Microsoft tested 12 shades of blue for Bing's link color. The winning shade earned an extra $80 million per year.

buildup.text: Each shade was shown to millions of users. Clicks, time on page, and revenue were tracked for every variation.

discovery.text: The optimal blue wasn't the prettiest or the most readable. It was the one that maximized click-through rate.

twist.text: No designer could have guessed which blue would win. The difference between shades was invisible to the naked eye.

climax.text: At massive scale, tiny differences compound. A 0.1% improvement across billions of searches means millions in revenue.

punchline.text: At scale, tiny improvements mean massive gains. Test everything.

quiz.question: How much extra revenue did the winning shade of blue generate for Bing?
